food_subgroup: Carbonated soft drink with fresh lemon

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No specific FoodOn entry for frozen lemon 7UP; closest parent is carbonated beverage (FOODON_03301710).
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 AN000A covers carbonated soft drinks as the closest parent category; no specific code for frozen lemon 7UP exists.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for this specific beverage preparation.

preparation_method: fresh lemon squeezed into carbonated drink

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard glass of approximately 350 ml, consisting of chilled 7UP carbonated soft drink with freshly squeezed lemon juice and ice, as typically served at a Hong Kong-style café or Singapore bing sutt.

nutrient_source.title: Carbonated soft drink, lemon-lime type (7UP / Sprite equivalent) with fresh lemon juice — estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy: Carbonated beverage, lemon-lime (NDB 14148) and USDA FoodData Central: Lemon juice, raw (NDB 09152)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Frozen Lemon 7UP is a Hong Kong / Singapore café drink made from 7UP carbonated lemon-lime soda and fresh lemon juice served over ice. No direct entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA for this exact preparation. Nutrients estimated by blending USDA SR Legacy NDB 14148 (carbonated lemon-lime beverage, ~90% of volume) and USDA NDB 09152 (lemon juice raw, ~10% of volume) to approximate a typical 90:10 ratio. Per-100g values reflect this blended estimate. Energy and sugar are dominated by the carbonated soda component; lemon juice contributes vitamin C and minor acidity.

gi_source.title: Fructose, sucrose and glucose-sweetened carbonated soft drinks — GI values from University of Sydney GI Database; lemon-lime carbonated soft drink analogous estimate
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Frozen Lemon 7UP. GI estimated at 59 based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for regular sucrose-sweetened carbonated soft drinks (lemon-lime type), which report GI values in the range 56–65 (medium). The lemon juice component (~10%) contributes negligible additional carbohydrate and does not materially alter the GI. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (59 × 34.3) / 100 ≈ 20.2, rounded to 20.1. Serving carbohydrate = 9.8 g/100g × 350 g serving = 34.3 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (~9 mg per 100 g; ~32 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Frozen Lemon 7UP is low in sodium and does not pose a direct hypertensive risk from salt load, but the high sugar content may contribute to weight gain and metabolic dysfunction that indirectly elevates blood pressure with regular consumption.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High free sugar content (~9.5 g per 100 g; ~33 g per 350 ml serving) and medium glycemic index (~59)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit Frozen Lemon 7UP due to its high free sugar load and medium GI, which can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation; a 350 ml serving delivers approximately 33 g of rapidly absorbed sugars.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High free sugar intake linked to elevated triglycerides and cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages such as Frozen Lemon 7UP is associated with elevated serum triglycerides and increased cardiovascular disease risk; individuals with existing CVD should limit intake to occasional consumption only.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Frozen Lemon 7UP (冻柠七, Dong Ning Qi) is a popular cold beverage originating from Hong Kong bing sutts (ice rooms / café-style eateries) and widely served in Singapore Chinese-style cafés and kopitiam settings. It is made by combining chilled 7UP lemon-lime carbonated soft drink with freshly squeezed lemon juice and crushed ice or ice cubes, resulting in a refreshing, tangy, and sweet sparkling drink. The name literally translates as 'frozen lemon seven' — 冻 (frozen/chilled), 柠 (lemon), 七 (seven, referring to 7UP). The drink is characterised by its bright citrus aroma, effervescent texture, and balance of sweetness from the soda and tartness from the fresh lemon. It is typically served in a tall glass and is a staple of Hong Kong-style café menus across Singapore. Nutritionally, Frozen Lemon 7UP is a high-sugar, low-nutrient beverage. Per 100 ml it provides approximately 38 kcal, 9.8 g carbohydrate (predominantly free sugars at ~9.5 g), negligible protein and fat, and a small amount of vitamin C (~4.5 mg) from the fresh lemon juice. A standard 350 ml serving delivers approximately 133 kcal and 33 g of free sugars, which is clinically significant. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 59 (medium), and the glycemic load per serving is approximately 20, making it a high-GL beverage. It is not suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes or those managing blood glucose, and regular consumption is associated with increased cardiovascular risk due to its free sugar content. It is naturally fat-free, low in sodium, and suitable for vegetarian, vegan, and halal diets.

confidence_reason: No direct entry for Frozen Lemon 7UP exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or any primary regional nutrition database. Nutrient values are estimated by blending USDA SR Legacy data for carbonated lemon-lime beverage (NDB 14148) and raw lemon juice (NDB 09152) in an approximate 90:10 ratio. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database using analogous carbonated lemon-lime soft drinks. Confidence is medium because the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible but not based on a direct laboratory measurement of this specific preparation.
notes: ZERO-VALUE JUSTIFICATIONS: fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy NDB 14148 (carbonated lemon-lime beverage) which reports 0 g fat; lemon juice (NDB 09152) also reports 0 g fat; fat_g is naturally zero for this beverage type. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g are all 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA source data for both components; naturally zero as this is a sugar-water carbonated beverage with no lipid-containing ingredients. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero; naturally zero as no animal-derived fat is present. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero; carbonated soft drinks contain no dietary fibre and lemon juice contributes negligible fibre at the 10% blend ratio, rounded to 0.0. SENTINEL VALUE (-1) JUSTIFICATIONS: The following micronutrients are set to -1 because they are not reported in the USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy source entries used for this estimation (NDB 14148 carbonated lemon-lime beverage and NDB 09152 raw lemon juice), and therefore cannot be reliably derived for this blended preparation: vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 — not reported in USDA SR Legacy NDB 14148 or NDB 09152 for this beverage type; sentinel value -1 used per Veritas convention. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 — not reported in USDA SR Legacy NDB 14148 or NDB 09152; sentinel value -1 used per Veritas convention. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 — not reported in USDA SR Legacy NDB 14148 or NDB 09152; sentinel value -1 used per Veritas convention. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 — not reported in USDA SR Legacy NDB 14148 or NDB 09152; sentinel value -1 used per Veritas convention. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 — not reported in USDA SR Legacy NDB 14148 or NDB 09152; sentinel value -1 used per Veritas convention. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 — not reported in USDA SR Legacy NDB 14148 or NDB 09152; sentinel value -1 used per Veritas convention. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 — not reported in USDA SR Legacy NDB 14148 or NDB 09152; sentinel value -1 used per Veritas convention. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 — not reported in USDA SR Legacy NDB 14148 or NDB 09152; sentinel value -1 used per Veritas convention. vitamin_e_mg is -1 — not reported in USDA SR Legacy NDB 14148 or NDB 09152; sentinel value -1 used per Veritas convention. phosphorus_mg is -1 — not reported in USDA SR Legacy NDB 14148 or NDB 09152; sentinel value -1 used per Veritas convention. magnesium_mg is -1 — not reported in USDA SR Legacy NDB 14148 or NDB 09152; sentinel value -1 used per Veritas convention. zinc_mg is -1 — not reported in USDA SR Legacy NDB 14148 or NDB 09152; sentinel value -1 used per Veritas convention. selenium_mcg is -1 — not reported in USDA SR Legacy NDB 14148 or NDB 09152; sentinel value -1 used per Veritas convention. OTHER NOTES: GI estimated at 59 from University of Sydney GI Database analogous carbonated soft drink entries; no direct GI measurement for this specific preparation exists. Serving size set at 350 g (approximately 350 ml) as a standard tall-glass café serving. Food is served at Keming Bing Sat 克茗冰室 (North Bridge Road, Singapore), a Hong Kong-style café. Price SGD 6.40 per serving as recorded from GrabFood Singapore source.
